questions.questionText: Gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM) is screened at:
questions.optionB: 24-28 weeks of gestation (75g OGTT — WHO/DIPSI, or 50g GCT followed by 100g OGTT — Carpenter-Coustan)
questions.explanation: GDM screening: 24-28 weeks (peak insulin resistance — HPL effect). DIPSI (India): 75g glucose non-fasting, 2-hr value ≥140 mg/dL = GDM. WHO: 75g OGTT (fasting ≥92, 1-hr ≥180, 2-hr ≥153 — any one). Carpenter-Coustan: 50g GCT screen → if ≥130-140 → 100g OGTT. Complications: macrosomia, shoulder dystocia, neonatal hypoglycemia, birth trauma, pre-eclampsia. Treatment: MNT (medical nutrition therapy) first → insulin if uncontrolled (metformin in some protocols). Postpartum: 75g OGTT at 6-12 weeks (40% develop T2DM). Reference: DC Dutta — GDM.

questions.questionText: Rh isoimmunization is prevented by giving Anti-D immunoglobulin to Rh-negative mother at:
questions.optionB: 28 weeks of gestation (antenatal prophylaxis) AND within 72 hours of delivery of Rh-positive baby, and after any sensitizing event
questions.explanation: Anti-D prophylaxis: prevents Rh isoimmunization in Rh-negative unsensitized mother. Standard: 300 μg (1500 IU) Anti-D IM. Timing: (1) Antenatal: 28 weeks (routine), (2) Postnatal: within 72 hours of delivery of Rh-positive baby, (3) After sensitizing events: abortion, ectopic, amniocentesis, CVS, APH, abdominal trauma. Kleihauer-Betke test: quantifies fetomaternal hemorrhage → determines if additional Anti-D needed. Already sensitized (positive ICT): Anti-D will NOT help → monitor with MCA-PSV Doppler, intrauterine transfusion if hydrops. Reference: DC Dutta — Rh Isoimmunization.
